source_metadata.title_raw: Samantha W. Zutler - A-18-209 - January 7, 2018 - Oakland

content.full_text:
STATE OF CALIFORNIA 
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION 
· 
1102 Q Street • Suite 3000 • Sacramento, CA 95811 
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886 
Samantha W. Zutler 
City Attorney 
City of Healdsburg 
1901 Harrison Street, Suite 900 
Oakland, CA 94612-3501 
Re: 
Your Request for Advice 
Our File No. A-18-209 
Dear Ms. Zutler: 
January 7, 2018 
This letter responds to your request for advice on behalf of City of Healdsburg Vice Mayor 
David Hagele regarding the conflict of interest provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act"). 1 
Please note that we are only providing advice under the conflict of interest provisions of the Act. In 
addition, please note that we are not a finder of fact when rendering advice (In re Oglesby (1975) 1 
FPPC Ops. 71 ), and any advice we provide assumes your facts are complete and accurate. If this is 
not the case or if the facts underlying these decisions should change, you should contact us for 
additional advice. 
Additionally, the Commission does not provide advice regarding past conduct. (Regulation 
18329(b )(8)(A).) Accordingly, nothing in this letter should be construed to evaluate any past 
conduct, and any conclusions contained in this letter apply only to prospective actions. 
QUESTION 
May Vice Mayor Hagele take part in City Council decisions relating to a multi-parcel 
development, given that he owns property within 500 feet of a portion of the development? 
CONCLUSION 
No. We do not find sufficient facts to indicate that there will be no reasonably foreseeable 
measurable impact on Vice Mayor Hagele's property and he is prohibited from making, 
participating in making, or using his positon to influence the listed decisions relating to the multi-
parcel development. 
1 The Political Reform Act is contained in Government Code Sections 8 I 000 through 91014. All statutory 
references are to the Government Code, unless otherwise indicated. The regulations of the Fair Political Practices 
Commission are contained in Sections 18110 through 18997 of Title 2 of the California Code of Regulations. All 
regulatory references are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations, unless otherwise indicated. 


FACTS AS PROVIDED BY THE REQUESTOR 
File No. A-18-209 
Page No. 2 
Saggio Hills Luxury Resort ("Saggio Hills") is a 258.5-acre development project in the City 
of Healdsburg. Saggio Hills is underway pursuant to a Development Agreement between the 
developer and the City. The City Council adopted the Development Agreement by ordinance. 
The approximate 250-acre lot was subdivided into 11 smaller parcels. Saggio Hills is 
expected to include a 130-unit resort hotel and associated amenities ( e.g., spa, restaurant, gift shop, 
demonstration cooking facility, outdoor wedding pavilion), 70 resort residences, a community park, 
public and private open space and trails, a pump station, and a City fire substation. While not part of 
the resort, the developer will also be dedicating a 12-acre portion of the property to the City for 
future development of up to 150 units of affordable housing. Saggio Hills is currently under 
development, but was formerly an open area. 
Vice Mayor Hagele's home sits within 500 feet of Saggio Hills. Specifically, his residence 
is located within 500 feet of Parcel 8 of Saggio Hills. Parcel 8 is designated for future construction 
of a community park and, pursuant to the Development Agreement, will be dedicated to the City by 
the developer. The City expects the park's dedication to occur in late 2019 or 2020. 
The City expects Saggio Hills visitors to enter through a separate entrance, independent 
from the surrounding residential neighborhood. In the future, visitors and residents will have 
primary access by the connection of Passalacqua Road and Parkland Farms Boulevard. This route 
will also provide access to the community park. At this time, the size of the affordable housing 
project has not been determined and no environmental analysis has been conducted, so impacts on 
the Parkland Farms neighborhood resulting from the future affordable housing portion of the project 
are not yet known. In an e-mail exchange on November 15, 2018, you stated that the City does not 
anticipate impacts to views, parking, noise, or privacy at Vice Mayor Hagele's property. 
The City Council anticipates future decisions regarding Saggio Hills, some of which 
include: 
• 
City acceptance of the Parcel 9 (pump station), and improvements; 
• 
Approval of water rate surcharge for operation and maintenance of pump station; 
• 
Amendment to the Development Agreement addressing timing and sequencing issues; 
• 
City acceptance of the Parcel 8 (community park); 
• 
Approval of master plan for community park; 
• 
Award of a construction contract for the community park; 
• 
City acceptance of the Parcel 10 (fire substation), and improvements; 
• 
City acceptance of Parcels 5 and 6 (affordable housing); 
• 
Approval of plans for the affordable housing project; 
• 
Approval of an agreement with a future developer for the affordable housing project; 
and 
• 
City acceptance of various public improvement projects that will be constructed by the 
developer (e.g., trails, streets, utilities). 


File No. A-18-209 
Page No. 3 
Separate from Saggio Hills, the City Council may, in var_ious contexts, consider an 
affordable housing plan for the entire community, including all of the property the City owns that 
could be used for affordable housing. Because the City would own Parcel 8, which is designated for 
use as affordable housing, the property will be included in any such discussions.2 
ANALYSIS 
Section 87100 prohibits any public official from making, participating in making, or 
otherwise using his or her official position to influence a governmental decision in which the 
official has a financial interest. A public official has a "financial interest" in a governmental 
decision if it is reasonably foreseeable that the decision will have a material financial effect on one 
or more of the public official's interests. (Section 87103; Regulation 18700(a).) Section 87103 
identifies interests from which a conflict of interest may arise, including the following which are 
relevant to the question presented: 
• 
An interest in real property in which the officer has a direct or indirect interest of $2,000 
or more. (Section 87103(b).) 
• 
The official's interest in his or her personal finances and those of immediate family 
members. (See Section 87103.) An official always has an interest in his or her personal 
finances. 
Assuming Vice Mayor Hagele has an interest of $2,000 or more in his residence, he has an 
interest in real property, as well as his personal finances. 3 
Foreseeability and Materiality 
The standard for foreseeability varies depending on whether an interest is explicitly 
involved in a decision. An official's financial interest is explicitly involved in a governmental 
decision, and is presumed to be reasonably foreseeable, if the interest is a named party in, or the 
subject of, a governmental decision before the official or the official's agency. (Regulation 
18701(a).)4 For a financial interest that is not explicitly involved in the decision at issue, the 
financial effect of the decision on an official's interest is reasonably foreseeable if it can be 
recognized as a realistic possibility and more than, hypothetical or theoretical. (Regulation • 
2 We note that we do not have sufficient information to fully analyze decisions regarding an affordable housing 
plan for all City owned property. However, to the extent that the plan implicates Saggio Hills, the Vice Mayor will be 
disqualified unless the decision can be segmented as provided in Regulation 18706 or the public generally exception 
applies under Regulation 18704. 
3 An effect on a public official's interest in his or her own personal finances, or those of the public official's 
immediate family, is not considered separately if the decision also affects the public official's interest in real property. 
(Regulation 18702.S(c).) As the decision at issue involves the Vice Mayor's interest in real property, we do not analyze 
his interest in his personal finances. 
4 A financial interest is the subject ofa proceeding if the decision involves the issuance, renewal, approval, 
denial, or revocation of any license, permit, or other entitlement to, or contract with, the financial interest, and includes 
any governmental decision affecting a real property financial interest as described in Regulation 18702.2(a)(l)-(6). 


File No. A-18-209 
Page No. 4 
18701(b).) Based on the provided facts, Vice Mayor Hagele's property is not explicitly involved in 
the decision at issue. 
For real property not explicitly involved in a decision, Regulation 18702.2(a) establishes 
that a foreseeable effect of a governmental decision on an official's financial interest is material if 
the decision: 
"(10) Would change the character of the parcel ofreal property by 
substantially altering traffic levels or intensity of use, including parking, of property 
surrounding the official's real property parcel, the view, privacy, noise levels, or air 
quality, including odors, or any other factors that would affect the market value of 
the real property parcel in which the official has a financial interest; 
"(11) Would consider any decision affecting real property located within 
500 feet of the property line of the official's real property, other than commercial 
property containing a business entity where the materiality standards are analyzed 
under Regulation 18702.1. Notwithstanding this prohibition, the Commission may 
provide written advice allowing an official to participate under these circumstances 
if the Commission determines that there are sufficient facts to indicate that there 
will be no reasonably foreseeable measurable impact on the official's property; or 
"(12) Would cause a reasonably prudent person, using due care and 
consideration under the circumstances, to believe that the governmental decision 
was of such a nature that its reasonably foreseeable effect would influence the 
market value of the official's property." 
Based on the provided facts, Vice Mayor Hagele's home is located within 500 feet of 
Saggio Hills. The Saggio Hills development includes, among other things, a 130-unit resort hotel, a 
spa, restaurants, retail, and an outdoor wedding pavilion. Additionally, the development expects to 
build 70 resort residences, a community park, public and private open spaces and trails, a pump 
station, and a fire substation. The developer is also expected to dedicate a 12-acre portion of the 
property for the future development of approximately 150 affordable housing units. The conversion 
of open space to a luxury resort with retail and roughly 220 new residential units could affect the 
market value of the real property in the surrounding area, which includes Vice Mayor Hagele's 
residence. Given the significant change to the character of the area surrounding Vice Mayor 
Hagele's residence, we do not find sufficient facts to indicate that there will be no reasonably 
foreseeable measurable impact on his property. 
Accordingly, Vice Mayor Hagele may not take part in decisions relating to Saggio Hills. 
Given the multitude of anticipated future decisions related to the Saggio Hills, should the City 
Council consider a decision that does not appear to impact Vice Mayor Hagele's financial interests, 
you may wish to request further advice. 


File No. A-18-209 
Page No. 5 
If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660. 
Sincerely, 
By: 
Counsel, Legal Division 
SKP:jgl
